title: Information for Laboratories | Influenza (Flu) | CDC
text:
Viral Genomic Sequencing Infrastructure 
View Menu 
Information for Laboratories 
Sept. 17, 2024 
What to know 
This page provides information and resources for laboratories and laboratory staff related to influenza testing and reporting. CDC works to help more than 400 clinical and public health laboratories perform tests in influenza and report results to CDC.
Information for Laboratory Directors and Staff 
Rapid Diagnostic Testing for Influenza: Information for Clinical Laboratory Directors 
A table listing available flu tests that are rapid and differentiate between influenza A and B. 
Guidance for Standards-Based Electronic Laboratory Reporting for Influenza 
Information on types of influenza lab tests, how to report results for each, and specific examples. 
Influenza Virus Testing Methods 
Table depicting Influenza virus testing methods. 
Influenza Specimen Collection Reference Guide 
This document is a reference guide for healthcare workers to use when collecting influenza specimens.

Download 
Information for Clinicians on Rapid Diagnostic Testing for Influenza 
Rapid influenza diagnostic tests (RIDTs) can help diagnose patients who may be sick with influenza. 
Influenza Specimen Collection Poster 
This document is a reference guide for healthcare workers to use when collecting influenza specimens.
